rulings.Case Title: PEBBLEBROOK HOTEL, LP ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 17, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, SET ONE is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to Interrogatory Nos. 1 and 2 as requested. Motion is GRANTED in part as to (1) Interrogatory No. 3 - the City to identify those documents sufficient to show the insufficient transfer tax bills and notices issued during the identified time period (as opposed to "all documents that refer or relate to" these notices) and (2) Interrogatory No. 5 - the City to provide information on the qualifications of persons identified in response to Interrogatory 4, consistent with SF Admin Code 67.24(c). Motion is otherwise denied. Request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PEBBLEBROOK HOTEL, LP ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 17, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED in part as to RFP No. 1 and RFP No. 2 - the City to produce documents sufficient to show publicly available information in Revenue and Taxation Code Section 408.1(c). Motion is GRANTED in part as to RFP No. 4, RFP No. 5, RFP No. 7, RFP No. 8, and RFP No. 36, subject to the understanding that the City will produce documents actually considered in the assessments at issue and identify them consistent with CCP 2031.280. Motion is otherwise denied. Request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PEBBLEBROOK HOTEL, LP ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 17, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Claim for Refund of Interest Paid fails to state facts to support a cause of action. Under San Francisco Business and Tax Regulation Code 1115.2(b) the interest accrues on the entire amount of the delinquent tax, not a part of it. To the extent Plaintiff argues that this provision is contrary to the state law, no authority is cited for this proposition. Claim for Refund-Special Tax fails to state facts to support cause of action. Johnson v. County of Mendocino (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 1017. Claim for Refund of Taxes - Commerce Clause fails to state facts to support a cause of action. Plaintiff points to no allegations in the Second Amended Complaint showing compliance with the Government Claims provisions. To the extent Plaintiff relies on Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, the portion of the claim attached to the RJN as Exhibit 7 quoted in the opposition, does not provide "specificity sufficient to enable the responsible City officials" to understand that the claim was under the interstate commerce clause and evaluate it under the relevant law especially in the context of the section of the Claim in which the quoted language is located. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 17, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR COURT TO SIGN STIPULATION AND JUDGMENT is GRANTED.
